Are film trailers spoiling movies?
April asked high ticket prices were ruining your visits the cinema. Yes, you said overwhelmingly. But something else has put strain relationship with the cinema and it's not the popcorn.
Have you ever gone your local cinema watch new film only feel you've seen before? know have. 'Spoilerific' film trailers are now often detailed seems hardly worth watching the movie itself.
Imagine the trailer for Casablanca told you whether Ingrid Bergman went off with Humphrey Bogart Paul Henreid the end. the trailer for Citizen Kane revealed just what the dying man meant when muttered the word 'Rosebud'.
And seems that even some film directors agree. Colin Trevorrow, director new film, Jurassic World, has said think that trailers have shown far more the film than would have wanted.
the last couple months, I've paid £17.50 ticket see two films I've been anticipating for some time. £17.50 sounds steep, but that was for iMAX and I'd still have paid more than £10 for 'normal' ticket.
But left the cinema unfulfilled, because felt like I'd seen them both six months before.
both cases the trailer revealed the entire structure the story, key plot twists and expensive action sequences. Throw few character deaths for good measure and you've got the basis significant chunk what you've paid your money for.
really did feel cheated what the studio had wanted see advance.
this just modern trend? checked out trailers for 1981's Raiders the Lost Ark, and 1964's Goldfinger get bigger picture.
Both revealed some well-known scenes (including Goldfinger's iconic laser and dialogue), but the plot basics were instead explained voiceover, rather than any especially huge visual giveaways.
Besides, the chance actually seeing these trailers was significantly lessened due the technology available the time release. Which got thinking further.
'If you don't like it, don't watch it', hear you cry. wish were that simple. the age the internet, exposure has increased tenfold.
Marketing campaigns target social media and television, while the days simple poster are gone. Why commission still image when you can display scenes from the film screen station other public place?
Not only that, but trailers are also forced upon you the cinema itself before other films. Without blindfold and soundproof booth hide you have little choice.
It's not that have problem with marketing and ads. But they're spoiling the experience. don't want see all the best bits wrapped into two minutes, six months ahead release.
Have you had film spoiled its Hollywood revealing too much desperate attempt put bums seats?
ticket prices ruin your film-going experience?
Something George mentioned earlier about Terminator trailer jogged memory about another film from the same franchise, Terminator Genisys.
promote the film, trailer had been released that included major plot twist ACTUALLY the trailer. Now, had heard about this trailer and had been purposely avoiding it. However, even when you try avoid spoilers, sometimes it's out your control. When went see Mad Max, they showed the afore-mentioned trailer and before realised, was too late. cannot unseen.
Even the director, Alan Taylor, was unhappy about the spoilers the trailer.
Genisys was [spoiler alert] ..... indeed half the inspiration for this convo. saw that article about Alan Taylor's thoughts the other day, but could only sympathise much the film was, opinion, travesty.
Have you seen the film now Ryan? what did you think the big 'reveal'? For film that had very little else going for that really did completely spoil the experience for me.
just can't understand why that decision was taken. You don't need resort something like that generate interest said twist that good then word mouth will spread.
For anyone interested, the trailer for the new Bond film was released today.

best year ever The Jamestown Comet.
Resolving for better new year.
Well, the holidays are upon once more and 2016 nearing end. It's safe say that the past year has certainly been filled with remarkable change. What did 2016 mean you? Was end beginning? Did you experience triumph tragedy?
you're like most, was mixture both. Sometimes it's hard discern the difference. What some see catastrophe, others view success. really depends your perspective. course, the New Year always good time make change for the better your own life.
But aware that most statistics show that only around percent resolutions continue past six months. the same time, people who set goal based resolution are percent more likely succeed than those who not.
However you it, order change your life for the better you have get and take the first step. And, sometimes attitude everything where life changes are concerned.
You have stay positive, dismiss the naysayers and keep motivated and moving forward. So, whether you start because resolution just because you think now good time, your potential success failure rests largely with your motivation.
Additionally, remember that you can't force someone else change either. they want different life, they have take the steps toward that end.
For example, suppose Lisa dating Mike and she's having tough time because addicted role-playing games. Lisa wants eventually marry Mike, but she cannot handle the gaming.
Lisa believes, however, that she can change him, help him see the error his ways. She believes that she can set resolution reform him with love rolling pin (just kidding, she doesn't bake she actually uses frying pan).
Mike never going change because someone else wants him so. wants quit playing will have for his own reasons. certainly psychologist, but know that for any New Year's resolution successful, the desire change has come from within.
may wrong, but think the motivation for change the start new year comes from deep desire all for fresh start. Most people want strive for something better, matter what our situation. course, there are still people out there who simply don't care and are either complacent resigned.
For some, the new year offers opportunity get "it" right whatever your particular "it" happens the time. any case, it's you make those choices and follow through. you don't, you have one blame but yourself.
You can get things moving creating list the things you want accomplish the coming year. For the record, I'd leave out lottery jackpot and focus more realistic expectations, like finishing your education pursuing career change.
Then figure out what takes reach those milestones and make plan get there (the "make plan" part pretty important). You will likely have great deal work and, for some least, long-term goal may require multiple short-term steps. patient, work hard and stay focused.
Incidentally, staying focused may indeed the biggest challenge you face. The aforementioned plan vital. Lay out, consistent, stick one step time, rather than trying everything all once, and you can successful.
New Year's resolution can either step towards improving your life can fruitless, frustrating endeavor that causes you stress and worry. all depends how committed you are the kind change you want your life. However you it, the choice rests with you.
Gery Deer independent columnist and business writer. Listen the Deer Headlines podcast free

Parenting Podcasts: You Aren't Alone!
When you follow someone online, you get glimpse into their life. Suddenly instead feeling alone, you feel validated your decisions, and heck you might even learn something along the way!
Parenting podcasts offer this, and can help remind parents that whatever problem they're facing, they're not alone.
Listening into shows, reading people's stories online, can feel like you're eavesdropping their lives. However being allowed peak in, you suddenly realise that lot things about parenting are universal.
all lot the things the same.
also lot things differently, and it's always fascinating hear how others live their lives, and parent their children.
Whether you agree disagree, still makes for great conversation.
The Parenting View one these shows that has just started up, promising get conversations going about all things parenting.
Maria Foy, creator and host the podcast The Parenting View, says lot parents can get disillusioned what they see online, but with show like this, suddenly you're exposed conversation that unfiltered." She also goes say "No two people parent the same, and everybody's life experiences are different which makes these kind conversations fascinating. When stop and listen others, learn; and knowledge power. can choose what with that information however want".
mentioned our "Podcasts Are The New Black" post, you can listen podcast anywhere, anytime. You not have sit down front computer it.
you could doing your laundry, going for walk, feeding your baby and still able tune and feel less alone parent.
head over Soundcloud and start checking out the podcasts available. you're interested, you can check out The Parenting View's own podcast HERE.
You can check out, and subscribe it, iTunes Spotify (coming soon).

Work "Sunspot", Sun and Reflection project the main atrium's roof light.
The atrium the school's central space,- "maelstrom" pupils that move around and and down the various floors. The space experienced living and varied because the changes daylight and the seasons. "Sunspot" shape the glass roof, inspired gas explosions, and placed according calculations the sun's movement. When the sun shining, the spot will spiral down the rotunda, all the way the ground floor floor and then spiral back again(in relation the path the sun).
contributes visualising the movement this space, but completely different pace the other users. Each day with slightly different from the preceding one.
The title this project was originally its working title, but decided keep it. slightly humoristic and bit pedagogical since what one sees through these objects like being the inside pair eyes. One gets association entering the mouth large fish whale.
The work made Fresnel lens the outside(towards the outside) making the world turn upside-down distort faces close-up. mirrored tube runs through the wall and that distorts even more, and the inner walls are large circular surfaces that have 'running' lights around the edge and changing circle colour like iris. the inside there are also lenticular films that diffuse the view depending how one moves through the vestibule.
LED-programming and technology delivered Jarle Henriksen.

Whether it's because I've experienced natural disaster because spent some formative years the Boy Scouts I'm not sure, but events half world away this week have re-enforced the notion that it's good "be prepared". When started using Trello for our product backlog earlier this year thought was probably overkill trying get automated backup our data, since was being operated Fog Creek who have great reputation the industry, but ploughed regardless and yesterday that preparation paid off.
normally use Trello during our Sprint Review recap user stories and acceptance criteria and one such meeting occurred the same time Fog Creek were valiantly trying keep the service going New York was battered Hurricane Sandy. Normally this wouldn't have mattered keep paper copies our scrum board, but for the first time sprints were doing our review Auckland, these weren't much use.
there were, mid review, far from home, story cards, and Trello going and down like whores draws. Compared what the poor people the United States were going through this was obviously not big deal, but with little bit contingency planning was trivial task fire Google Drive, open our Trello backup spreadsheet and continue with our review.
This outage and the ongoing benefit get from being able print story cards from the backup spreadsheet means that has totally repaid the time spent writing the script you're using Trello for user stories, and you haven't done already then set the script now there's nothing lose and everything gain.
It's also timely reminder look all your digital data and think about what would happen you didn't have access it, worse still, lost permenantly. your house burned down fire, would you lose all the photos your kids? your favourite web service went under (financially rather literally) and took your data with them, then what would the impact be?
doesn't take much get yourself good shape get good automated offsite backup use Crashplan) handle your files, and periodically back your online data another service, even local and you should good go.
Oh, and you happen live the pacific ring fire, tornado alley, somewhere hot and dry, indeed anywhere our rapidly warming planet that has "weather" you might want generally prepared that any eventuality, you manage Get Thru.

her favourite colour purple knew had use that colour. month ago found some purple sugar and white sugar pearls. wanted kid cake that was elegant given her age. That was far got until the morning the day everyone was coming. Talk about decorating block! just couldn't think anything. Fortunately the walk with the dogs got idea. And had done quickly had hour this. Yikes!
Not only does she like purple, she likes butterflies decided with that. whipped some buttercream icing like did for these cupcakes. Before added the purple scooped some white into decorating bag.
Carefully place the top layer and brush any loose crumbs off.
Ice the whole cake smoothing best you can. Isn't this great purple?
Next used the white frosting outline the flowers and butterfly. Finally got the consistency right and flowed like should. the past was squeezing with all might. Too much work. used the white pearls for the centers and the purple sugar for the petals and wings. really need problem solve getting the sugar where want go. There was bit colouring outside the lines with this bit.
finish off added some icing dots the sides.

 cake remember always.

African/Lake Malawi cichlids have very specialised feeding habits that have been adapted that they can survive their natural habitat. However aquarists tend make easier for themselves getting readily prepared flakes and pellets. But you will have take into account whether your fish are herbivores 'piscivores' (feed other fish). Mbuna species are normally herbivore, they will mainly feed the algae that grows rocks, they must fed plant food. These fish cannot digest animal fat and will suffer from indigestion fed it.
the other hand most Lake Malawi cichlids will eat small crustaceans like Brine Shrimps.
lot cichlid breeders make their own homemade food, made from chopped shrimp and peas.
Thaw out the shrimps and peas and mince them finely food mixer, then add the vitamin drops. Finally pour the dissolved gelatine. The gelatine will bind the mixture, producing thick paste after has cooled. Place the paste into freezer bag, flatten and then freeze. Now this nutritious fish food can easily cut into convenient pieces, and given your fish.
wide range foods are commercially available, like flakes and pellets. has been noticed that cichlids feed very flakes, while pellets sink the bottom, any other inhabitants like catfish can get it. The choice yours.
further important source food for your African cichlids, are frozen foods like white and black mosquito larvae, brine shrimps and krill.
Mixes including beef heart and frozen RED mosquito larvae are not suitable for Lake Malawi Cichlids.
How often you feed your Lake Malawi cichlids depends their age. Fry (Newly born fish) must fed more frequently and smaller portions than older fish. Fry should literally swimming food order grow quickly and develop properly.
Herbivores are more used feeding small portions over the day, feeding them once day will not enough. And you must take care only put the right amount food. should take about minute for all the food eaten.
Adult cichlids should fed 2-3 times day while the fry should fed 5-7 times day. the fish are fed regularly then their aggression should reduced.
Never feed your fish just after you have switched the light they will need reach their full levels daytime activity. The African cichlids rest night and takes them about thirty minutes become fully active. And for similar reasons, you should not give them their final feed just before turning the light out.
you don't think that you will able feed your fish the correct times each day, then you should consider installing automatic feeding machine.
important not overfeed your fish your cichlids will consume anything, and huge quatities they can, which can harm their health. Healthy African Cichlids will always await their food eagerly.
adult cichlid eating poorly refusing food then don't feed them all for one full day afterwards may overfed. they still fail eat after the day off, then they may have contracted disease.
They are. Were they cichlids tropical fish? Thank you for your comment the way.
love aquarium fish and used keep quite few them. They are lot fun and beautiful watch.

Remember however that life has given you the experiences you need proceed your evolutionary process and that you have probably gained much internally from all this.

Are you thinking about using online assignment help platforms? you answered yes, then you are definitely not alone. This service increasing popularity across the globe. makes easier for students handle their school requirements. But the nature the service remains shrouded controversy. After all, teachers and professors give out assignments for reason. They are meant boost the skills students both inside and outside the classroom. This begs the question: assignment help services benefit hurt students?
It's easy see the benefits hiring assignments experts aid your academic pursuits. They solve the problem insufficient time homework's, especially you take multiple part-time jobs after school hours. you know, writing essays completing complicated assignments can take boatload time. Between gathering information and adding the final touches, your assignment can take several hours finish.
The speed with which you can finish your assignments with the help service providers like the guys Assignments another huge benefit. Most companies accept expedited requests for extra fee. It's possible get your paper assignment little hours. While these aforementioned benefits are surely hard resist, it's worth noting that using assignment writing services offers more than meets the eye.
first, might not appear make sense that hiring assignment help providers can improve your research skills. You don't even need research about anything. They will everything for you, and this where you can get tips from the experts.
looking the final output the assignment expert, you could see how much differs from the homework's you have done yourself. shouldn't come surprise these differences are very noticeable. You could tell that time and effort has been dedicated producing such well-researched assignment. Even the references and links can help you with your future requirements, you would see how much more credible your work will look like the links aren't limited Wikipedia.
The same applies your presentation skills. The way the essay, research paper, assignment formatted can give you ideas how better structure your content. Notice how the ideas flow smoothly throughout, allowing you get the point across even without the use fancy words. You probably have "go-to" format for your essays, but you'll realize that doesn't always work.
It's easy dismiss assignment writing services only for lazy students who not care all about learning. But it's important understand that the students can enjoy much more than its time-saving benefits. With the help experts, you can pick some tips how research about topic, what credible sources use, and how present your ideas way that engages readers. All these can prove beneficial for any student, even the ones who remarkably well class.

was brimming with excitement all Thursday. couldn't stop thinking about date with Seabiscuit. was very excited about his joining the theater plus the night adventures that would follow.
hurried home after work, took quick shower, and headed out. had been warm day, and was doubtful that needed jacket; however, then remembered being cold previous performance the evening. Even though had checked the weather and saw Orinda was only predicted the low 60s the evening, knew that the hills, where the theater actually located, can get much colder. felt foolish carrying such large jacket BART and felt too warm need it, but better safe than sorry.
was good did. the time arrived the theater, was significantly cooler. was happy without jacket but realized that once the sun finished setting, would need put on. waited for Seabiscuit arrive, and was excited see him clean-shaven-as had ordered his Mistress. How good that should obey well! And finally looked like one his own photographs.
told him previously that appeared servant the Many-Faced God because his face never seemed match from one photograph another. The angles were off something seemed strange about it. didn't look like his photographs. knew was him, but how strange and different they all seemed.
There was crowd, and could tell felt bit shy. hugged and kissed each other's cheeks, then held hands walked the hill from Will Call the theater.
bought dinner for the café, and continued holding hands. The line was long, and really only had minutes eat before needing seated. was impressed tickets and how close were the stage. The temperature had dropped significantly, put jacket when were seated but was astounded that didn't close until after intermission. held his hand his lap during the first half.
During intermission, rented blanket while used the restroom and got free bag Peet's coffee for renewing subscription for the theater. shared the blanket, and for the second half, held hand lap.
The performance was amazing, all the shows have seen there have been. CalShakes has done very well selecting and directing plays that resonate with race issues going currently, and this play was exceptionally well done that regard. Seabiscuit enjoyed greatly, and told him should return with for all next season. said would love to. was his first time going the theater very long time, and said the final scenes with the bed stage would linger his memory for long time.

part Living Thailand, the Rice Field Project follows our journey buying land, preparing for building our house and finally building our house. This part 34, read the other parts click The Rice Field Project. you are following this project then you might remember that had water well made our land. Well will later build guest bungalow other part our land had the idea have second water well made. The new one closer our house and use the old one for the garden and guest-bungalow. But that did not easy thought.
The first try was about meter from our house, they found water meter but not enough pump up. Second try was about meter from the first water well. But water all was found. third try was done about meter next the first water well. And bingo .... water. And lot water.
the location the first water well was just sheer luck? Anyway will see how works out with water wells near each other. The job was done the same people the first water well. And although they had try times, only paid for water well, 3.500 Thai baht some tip for the hard work. Because yes hard work, especially 30°C!
Update And did just paid the final payment for the house construction. The last will paid days after 100% completion and delivery the house.

There are few moments life this fast paced, that take you this much surprise. Where after the adrenaline high, you can't believe just happened you. This their story. sure watch the video well browse through the photos mom tells what happened from her perspective.
Ben's parents were already the hospital for induction. Last checked, she was dilated and not active labour all. The induction medication hadn't even begun. normally come when client active labour this early period could slowed down presence. texted with dad who told would touch base hour. texted see how things were going. Heard nothing for about minutes and just had this gut feeling. threw scrubs. gear was already the house and childcare had been called in. knew would today just not sure when.
Utter disbelief. What was talking about? She wasn't even labour. This was first baby. Complete completely dilated and 10? Maybe dad wasn't familiar with the terms.
put keys pocket.
screamed childcare gotta go, bye, I'm walking out, you are duty, bye" and walked right the car. was driving away minute later.
The drive had heart pumping like never before. knew this could either way, some first time moms push for one two hours might have time. Some deliver with couple pushes. parked exactly minutes from when walked out door (went back and checked all text time stamps).
ran maternity and heard baby crying from delivery room. heart sank. knew.
was laying her chest and was the first natural birth that ever beat it!
This was precipitous labor, which defined labor that lasts more than three hours from onset regular contractions delivery. Maybe you could call hers
The room less, was full peace, and with the first minute being there began shooting. The looks disbelief and utter joy were plastered all over their faces.
Ben's parents have graciously allowed share their birth part glorify God publicly for his miraculous life. The images chosen, many that are not the video, selected almost all black and white because this case, its just visually stunning when laid together. case you are wondering, clients receive all their images colour and black white for every session.
Ben's parents, the joy and graciousness you carry exudes from deep place knowing truth. What gift your son has having you both raise him. Thank you for hiring me. I'm slightly terrified you hire again that might just camp outside your house for week.
the hospital staff and Dr. Brandt Netcare Blaauwberg Hospital, sincerest thank you's for letting run all out breath and document their day.

Home "featured"Who are we... REALLY?
Psalm Identity, Who are we?
Regardless its few many letters?
what have yet still accomplish?
Has God our Father deemed limited any respect?
who are called be?
Our Lord has made His image- leaving nothing out.
leaves healing, mercy, goodness and kindness our wake.
That old thick and tainted sin!
Who has been set free?
Not many people take the time think about their "wake" and what they leave behind themselves. The Bible shows that Peter was filled with power and who was Christ that his very shadow healed the sick! psalms read that goodness and lovingkindness follow everywhere go! The word for today word encouragement, encouraging you not busy moving ahead that you bulldoze your way through life, leaving chaos and stresses behind you everywhere you go!
Who are we, really? are followers Christ all His actions and follow Him forward leave Him behind everywhere go!
God bless you friends you seek out the new and whole identity the follower Christ!

Why Many Businesses Choose Incorporate Nevada.
Nevada very attractive option for businesses choosing incorporate. Here are list the top reasons consider even your business not based Nevada.
Nevada Protects the Corporate Veil.
Nevada offers it's corporations great protection for its board directors, even you are the only member the board and own all the shares. the last years the Nevada courts have only allowed the corporate veil pierced twice, and both were for outright fraud cases. comparison, one out very two cases California.
Nevada does not exchange information with the IRS.
The only other state this Texas. Keep mind that you must register your corporation another state, the state laws there will apply.
Currently the filing fees with Nevada are $125.
Nevada offers protection Board Directors from shareholder lawsuits.
The shareholders must prove gross negligence order find the Board Directors liable. And prove gross negligence pierce the corporate veil Nevada.
Nevada, you must only have legal purpose form corporation LLC.
Meaning, person could form Nevada LLC hold personal assets. not required that the LLC have business purpose form.
There joint and several liabilities.
This means that judgment entered against several defendants, they will each assume equal liability for the full amount the judgment, regardless their relative fault causing the problem. Nevada, courts are required assign percentage faults each defendant. And each defendant only liable for their percentage.
One person can hold all the corporate positions.
Nevada Protects the Board Directors and Officers.
1987, Nevada passed law permitting corporations place their own provisions their Articles Incorporation that can eliminate the personal liablity the officers and directors the stockholders. Furthermore, this provision automatic Nevada.
Members are not required listed state records.
Stockholders, directors, and officers are not required citizens live hold meetings Nevada.

Everyone likes liked. This simple, human truth: mammals, are driven desire bond with others, feel accepted, and acknowledged for job well done. fact, research from the University Iowa shows that feeling accepted more motivating than even financial incentives and improves our productivity and performance. But there's difference between wanting and needing others' approval.
If, for example, you find yourself obsessing over the number "likes" you get social media forever waiting for your supervisor coworkers notice that you've done something well, you might caught toxic cycle approval addiction.
Approval addiction constant need for acceptance and approval that drives your daily actions the detriment your own needs, says Lynn Taylor, author Tame Your Terrible Office Tyrant: How Manage Childish Boss Behavior Thrive Your Job.
Approval addicts usually wind worn out and disappointed, adds psychologist Linda Sapadin, author Master Your Fears: How Triumph Over Your Worries and Get With Your Life. They spend much energy seeking positive feedback that they have less energy take care themselves work toward their meaningful goals. And, Taylor says, constantly seeking approval can cause people become workaholics, suffer health problems, and make choices that result less-than-ideal consequences.
The first step build awareness and adjust your priorities accordingly. begin, ask yourself some tough questions. What are your primary goals? What you want do? Then evaluate how you spend your time: Are you neglecting your dreams doing too much for others? With this awareness, you can deliberately choose activities that support your own needs, Taylor says, and step out the people-pleasing cycle.
Use the insights gleaned from your personal audit establish some ground rules and boundaries for yourself. Spend more time the things that leave you feeling positive and focused your dreams, needs, and values. Say "yes" the tasks and activities that align with those goals and don't afraid say, "No, but thanks for thinking me," those that don't, Sapadin adds. These guidelines will help you manage your time and energy way that leaves plenty left over help others you choose.
"If your objective set your own standards and appreciate your accomplishments and self-worth, you can 100 percent successful," Sapadin says. "You can set realistic goals and monitor them based your own criteria." And that goes long way building not only sense self-worth, but sense self-efficacy (the belief that you are capable solving task problem), resilience, and even purpose. You can have that right now and you don't need anyone's approval get it.
Polly Campbell has been speaking and writing about psychology, resilience, and wellness topics for more than years. She the author three books: How Live Awesome Life: How Live Well. Good. Happy; Imperfect Spirituality: Extraordinary Enlightenment for Ordinary People; and How Reach Enlightenment. Tweet her @PLCampbell.

Hand Hope's short-term medical and dental outreaches began with one trip Cambodia 2007. What started out small has now led several yearly trips countries around the world. Recently, our team was able complete the 100th short-term medical and dental outreach Zambia. Here are some the lives you were able impact the span just one day!
Oscar came our medical tent reporting that was randomly falling down every month. Just the night before, had fallen down and sustained traumatic injury his right arm. The doctor said that was the type fracture that could have very easily severed his main artery his arm. After Oscar's examination, was determined that had seizure disorder. Unfortunately, had way getting the hospital for treatment, paid for his transport. diagnosing his disorder, our team hoped could avoid future injuries his body.
Sweet Grace came our outreach hunched over and suffering for aches that most 89-year-olds would experience. However, she did not have the simple medicines available help. Our team was able give her aspirin, blood pressure medicine and pills for her muscles; such simple solutions the pain she was feeling. Our team was also happy pray with Grace before she left; "Now after prayer, feel that body has become normal. doesn't feel badly before." She left standing much straighter!
67-year-old Alex had been having trouble with his eyes for least two years. was not able see clearly, and which caused him difficulty when reading. After meeting with one our volunteers, received pair glasses and was ecstatic able read again. What was the first book picked up? One Joyce's from the prayer tent!
We've been able treat 297,923 patients and see 106,056 salvations over the course 100 trips, but there are many more lives reach! "Do you really think you're perfect?" The preacher asked.
Some people act like they're perfect. our faulty memory thinks our loved ones are were perfect.
recently read facebook post friend who was told fellow Christian that rarely sinned. opined that was not sinner, but saint. Actually, friend observed can both sinners and saints the same time. Saints can and sin.
Christians are not perfect, but are pardoned. We're not sinless, but we're sanctified. We're not faultless, but we're forgiven. learn that our failures don't have final fatal.
might well remember that some God's greatest heroes failed. Some miserably.
Yet, they didn't quit. They didn't allow failure define them, discourage them defeat them. Their failures were forgiven. And they pressed toward the prize. That's what must do.
must willing admit our failures. Confess our shortcomings. And repent our sins. are work progress. Ever striving conformed Christ instead our culture (Rom. 8:29; 12:1-2).
The late and legendary basketball coach John Wooden reminds us, "Failure isn't fatal, but failure change might be." the great British statesman, Winston Churchill observed, "Success not final, failure not fatal: the courage continue that counts." The Christian life about change. Growth. And persistence.
Like the apostle Peter, can begin again. can put together the broken pieces. Refocus our eyes Jesus. And even with our past flaws restored faithful service. Let pray David did: "Restore the joy your salvation, and uphold with willing spirit" (Ps 51:12).
There hope. There help. There healing.
There forgiving and gracious God.
